items.title: Nostalgia for an Age Yet to Come: Alienation, Neo-Emotions, and Radical Politics in Contemporary Capitalism
items.author: Ian Burkitt1School of Social Sciences, 1905University of Bradford, Bradford, UK
items.content: <article>Emotion Review, Ahead of Print. This article analyzes the emotions of alienation, estrangement, and nostalgia, and how they are central to the production of neo-emotions in contemporary society. Alienation is a product of the macro-social context of capitalist societies and contemporary ...</article>

items.title: Emotions and Societal Changes: The Nature and Social Functions of “Neo-Emotions”
items.author: Agneta H. Fischer1University of Amsterdam, Amsterdam, the Netherlands
items.content: <article>Emotion Review, Ahead of Print. Neo-emotions are defined by one shared characteristic: they are a response to macro-societal changes. To gain full insight into when neo-emotions occur, a sociological approach needs to be complemented with a psychological perspective to conceptualize the ...</article>

items.title: Feeling Some Type of Way: Black Feeling and Emotional Ambiguity

items.title: Empathic Perception: A Neutral Framework for Understanding Empathy
items.author: Rosemary Elizabeth Kirkpatrick, Stephen Jonathan Whitty, Anita Louise Wheeldon
items.content: <article>Emotion Review, Ahead of Print.
